'''Jason Qareqare''' ({{IPA|fj|ŋɡareŋɡare}}; born 26 January 2004) is a Fijian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan or dual registration at [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]], and at [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in [[RFL League 1]].

==Background==
Qareqare was born in [[Lautoka, Fiji]]. As the son of a [[British Army]] soldier, he lived in [[Osnabrück, Germany]] for four years before his family relocated to [[Yorkshire]] in 2008.<ref name="TYP Schoolboy">{{Cite web|last=Smith|first=Peter|date=2022-01-04|title=Castleford Tigers: Schoolboy Jason Qareqare ready to make the grade in Super League|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-schoolboy-jason-qareqare-ready-to-make-the-grade-in-super-league-3514926|access-date=2022-02-01|website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

Growing up, he previously played [[Association football|football]] with [[York City]] and [[rugby union]] with Ripon RUFC and school teams. He was inspired to take up [[rugby league]] by {{Rlnt|Fiji}}'s run to the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] semi-finals as well as due to the sport's popularity in the area.<ref name="TYP Schoolboy" /><ref>{{Cite web|last=Nasiko|first=Repeka|date=2021-06-26|title=Jason hits headlines – Sacrifice and commitment pays off for youngster|url=https://www.fijitimes.com/jason-hits-headlines-sacrifice-and-commitment-pays-off-for-youngster/?fbclid=IwAR3qVcsguGI_L3-VpR3ok_ZBker74qQ3boxKyRQ2_WdU7nbJ_COdi_77s7M|access-date=2022-02-01|website=The Fiji Times}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=2022-01-05 |title=Jason Qareqare: How Castleford Tigers youngster was inspired by Fiji's performance at 2013 Rugby League World Cup |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/shocked-tigers-prospect-qareqare-stunned-by-rapid-rise-3514101 |access-date=2022-11-03 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref>

Qareqare attended [[Sherburn High School]]. He played his junior rugby league for the [[Kippax Knights]] before signing for the [[Castleford Tigers]] academy aged 14.<ref name="YL Union Scholarship">{{Cite web |last=Heppenstall |first=Ross |date=14 June 2021 |title=Jason Qareqare's remarkable journey to Castleford Tigers - meet the Fijian on a union scholarship |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jason-qareqare-castleford-tigers-fiji-20812594 |access-date=13 September 2023 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref> He joined [[Queen Ethelburga's Collegiate]] on a rugby union [[scholarship]], where he studied Maths, Sport and Business, while training part-time with Castleford.<ref name="YL Union Scholarship" />

His younger brother, [[Marcus Qareqare]], is also a rugby league player and progressed through the [[Leeds Rhinos]] academy ranks.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=21 May 2024 |title=The Super League club Jason Qareqare turned down to pen extension with Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.totalrl.com/the-super-league-club-jason-qareqare-turned-down-to-pen-extension-with-castleford-tigers/ |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Castleford Tigers===
====2021–22====
On 10 June 2021, 17-year-old Qareqare was given a late call-up into the [[Castleford Tigers]] side to face [[Hull FC]]. Having been expected to play for the under-19s that weekend,<ref name="YL Union Scholarship" /><ref name="Sky Debut Try">{{Cite web |date=10 June 2021 |title=Jason Qareqare: Castleford Tigers debut try was 'an out-of-this-world feeling' despite loss to Hull FC |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12329838/jason-qareqare-castleford-tigers-debut-try-was-an-out-of-this-world-feeling-despite-loss-to-hull-fc |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> he received a phone call from first team head coach [[Daryl Powell]] while in maths class at school, to tell him he would make his [[Super League]] debut.<ref name="Sky Debut Try" /><ref>{{Cite web |date=10 June 2021 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 12-30 Hull FC |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57412052 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He started on the left wing and, inside the first minute, rounded Hull fullback [[Jake Connor]] to score a try with his first touch of the ball.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Wells |first=Jon |date=14 June 2021 |title=Jon Wells: A debut to remember for Castleford Tigers teenager Jason Qareqare |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12332662/jon-wells-a-debut-to-remember-for-castleford-tigers-teenager-jason-qareqare |access-date=13 September 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-12-hull-fc-30-hull-success-but-castleford-tigers-teenager-jason-qareqare-shines-on-debut-3269190 |title=Castleford Tigers 12 Hull FC 30: Hull success but Castleford Tigers teenager shines on debut |publisher=The Yorkshire Post |first=Dave |last=Craven |date=10 June 2021}}</ref> This was nominated for Super League Try of the Month,<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Super League|author-link=Super League|user=SuperLeague|number=1414578037006295042|title=An unforgettable debut try. Is Jason Qareqare's try for @CTRLFC your @northernassist Try of the Month for June?}}</ref> and was later voted Castleford's Try of the Year despite strong competition.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-10-04|title=2021 End Of Season Awards|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7656|access-date=2022-02-01|website=Castleford Tigers|archive-date=1 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220201184527/https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7656|url-status=dead}}</ref> He went on to make two further appearances in 2021, featuring off the bench against [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] and starting at fullback in a youthful side against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=16 June 2021 |title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 12-18 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57473065 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=11 July 2021 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 18-70 Salford Red Devils - Salford thrash youthful Cas side |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57781068 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Qareqare signed a two-year contract extension with Castleford in April 2022.<ref>{{Cite web |date=19 April 2022 |title=Qareqare signs extension with Cas |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8006 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=19 April 2022 |title=Jason Qareqare: Castleford Tigers winger signs contract extension |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61155643 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In his first start of the [[2022 Castleford Tigers season|2022 season]] on 22 April, he once again scored a long-range try in the first minute with his first touch of the ball, beating four [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] defenders on the touchline.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-04-22 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 30-10 St Helens - Cas too good for Saints youngsters |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61164515 |access-date=2022-11-03 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> This score went on to be voted Super League Try of the Month, and in December was voted Super League Try of the Season.<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Super League|author-link=Super League|user=SuperLeague|number=1525098543991726080|title=You've voted Jason Qareqare's stunning try as April's @northernassist Try of the Month}}</ref><ref>{{cite tweet|author=Super League|author-link=Super League|user=SuperLeague|number=1606666006092402688|title=The 2022 @northernassist Try of the Season - Congratulations, @CTRLFC's Jason Qareqare}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=24 December 2022 |title=Super League Try of the Season awarded to Castleford Tigers star for incredible effort against St Helens |url=https://www.totalrl.com/super-league-try-of-the-season-awarded-to-castleford-tigers-star-for-incredible-effort-against-st-helens/ |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> On 15 May, he scored two tries in Castleford's victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=15 May 2022 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 32-0 Hull KR - Six-try Cas dismantle scoreless Rovers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61380041 |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> The following week, Qareqare scored his second consecutive brace against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], with his "pace and skill in tight situations evidenced by his sublime finishes".<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 May 2022 |title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 30-14 Castleford Tigers - Burgess scores treble as Salford see off Cas |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61523411 |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He finished the season with 7 tries in 8 appearances.<ref name="RLRKC" />

=====Whitehaven (loan)=====
On 22 July 2022, Qareqare joined [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan, alongside teammate [[Cain Robb]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=22 July 2022 |title=Jason Qareqare makes Championship move on loan |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jason-qareqare-makes-championship-move-on-loan |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=22 July 2022 |title=Castleford Tigers starlet Jason Qareqare joins Championship club on loan |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-starlet-jason-qareqare-joins-championship-club-on-loan/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220722191145/https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-starlet-jason-qareqare-joins-championship-club-on-loan/ |archive-date=22 July 2022 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> However, the following week, they were both recalled to Castleford without having made an appearance, as the Tigers were unable to name a full 21-man squad due to mounting injury issues.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=27 July 2022 |title=Castleford recall Jason Qareqare days after loaning winger to Championship side |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-recall-jason-qareqare-days-after-loaning-winger-to-championship-side |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=====Midlands Hurricanes (dual registration)=====
Qareqare made two appearances for the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in the [[RFL League 1]] in 2023, through their dual registration agreement with Castleford.<ref name="RLRKC" />

=====Bradford Bulls (loan)=====
On 20 April 2023, it was announced that Qareqare had joined the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] on an initial two-week loan deal, continuing on a rolling basis.<ref>{{cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=20 April 2023 |title=Castleford winger Jason Qareqare makes Championship loan move |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jason-qareqare-castleford-bradford-loan |accessdate=20 April 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> Tigers head coach [[Andy Last]] explained the move; "Jason is a player with huge potential and we want to create a pathway for him. Jason needs to work on some aspects of his game and getting some consistent rugby at Bradford will stretch him a little bit more than the reserves."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=20 April 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Andy Last explains Jason Qareqare's move to Bradford Bulls |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-boss-andy-last-explains-jason-qareqares-move-to-bradford-bulls/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230420114108/https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-boss-andy-last-explains-jason-qareqares-move-to-bradford-bulls/ |archive-date=20 April 2023 |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref> In June, having made two appearances, he was recalled to Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=2 June 2023 |title=Castleford coach lays down challenge for Jason Qareqare with starlet recalled from loan |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-coach-lays-down-challenge-for-jason-qareqare-with-starlet-recalled-from-loan |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

====2023–26====
For the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023 season]], Qareqare switched from squad number 33 to 31.<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 January 2023 |title=Tigers announce squad numbers for 2023 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8438 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers |archive-date=23 August 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230823002850/https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8438 |url-status=dead }}</ref> After being recalled from loan in June, he scored the late winning try for Castleford in their 26–24 victory against [[Leeds Rhinos]] at the [[Magic Weekend#2023: Newcastle|2023 Magic Weekend]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=3 June 2023 |title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos 24-26 Castleford - Tigers end six-game losing run |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65754053 |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Bazeley |first=Marc |date=3 June 2023 |title=Leeds Rhinos 24-26 Castleford Tigers: Jason Qareqare's converted try snatches vital Magic Weekend win |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leeds-vs-castleford/58333 |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He scored again in the two following rounds against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 June 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 10-42 Salford Red Devils - Visitors move into top four after win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65822021 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=24 June 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 23-14 Warrington Wolves - Home side hold off comeback |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65956831 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 25, Qareqare scored two tries for Castleford in their 29–12 victory over [[Hull FC]].<ref>{{cite web |date=8 September 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 29-12 Hull FC - Cas close in on survival |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66729971 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=8 September 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers 29 Hull FC 12: Jubilant hosts on brink of safety after precious win |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-29-hull-fc-12-jubilant-hosts-on-brink-of-safety-after-precious-win-4286661 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> The second of these, a length-of-the-field interception from a Hull scrum play, went on to be voted Castleford's Try of the Season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 September 2023 |title=Tigers host end of season dinner |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8761 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers |archive-date=21 September 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230921100638/https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8761 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

In [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024]], Qareqare started in the opening rounds but suffered an ankle injury on 25 February, expected to keep him out for two months.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=29 February 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Craig Lingard confirms serious injury blow with replacement confirmed for Warrington Wolves clash |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-boss-craig-lingard-confirms-serious-injury-blow-with-replacement-confirmed-for-warrington-wolves-clash/ |access-date=25 March 2024 |website=TotalRL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=7 March 2024 |title=Breaking down Castleford Tigers' squad situation after more key injury blows |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/breaking-down-castleford-tigers-squad-situation-after-more-key-injury-blows |access-date=25 March 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> On 17 May, Castleford announced that Qareqare had signed a further two-year contract extension.<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 May 2024 |title=Qareqare and Hill extend Castleford contracts |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cgrwlz857w5o |access-date=17 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=17 May 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers tie down exciting winger Jason Qareqare to continue 2025 plans |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-tie-down-exciting-winger-jason-qareqare-to-continue-2025-plans-4632327 |access-date=17 May 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> The following week, on his return from injury against Hull in round 12, he scored a try and assisted another for [[Tex Hoy]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 May 2024 |title=Milnes pulls strings as Castleford see off Hull FC |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cw99x3g2w88o |access-date=20 April 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored two tries against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in round 19 and repeated this feat against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in round 22.<ref>{{Cite web |date=28 July 2024 |title=Salford deny Castleford to boost play-off hopes |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/crgrp51qgl1o |access-date=20 April 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=18 August 2024 |title=Huddersfield edge out Cas at Magic Weekend |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cgkygn37gk4o |access-date=20 April 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Qareqare was rested for one game in August as a precautionary measure for a tight hamstring,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=2 August 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers coach delivers Jason Qareqare injury update following Leigh Leopards defeat |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-coach-delivers-jason-qareqare-injury-update-following-leigh-leopards-defeat |access-date=20 April 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> but otherwise featured in every round from May, making 17 appearances.

Qareqare was promoted to Castleford's number 2 for the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=6 December 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2025 squad numbers as new-look spine formed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2025-squad-numbers-as-new-look-spine-formed |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> During pre-season training, he sustained a hamstring injury which forced him to miss the opening two months of the campaign.<ref>{{cite tweet |author=BBC Sport West Yorkshire |user=BBCWYS |number=1885316726125899802 |title=Qareqare ruled out for Tigers!}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=27 February 2025 |title=Danny McGuire offers positive Rowan Milnes update with return timeline outlined |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-mcguire-offers-positive-rowan-milnes-update-with-return-timeline-outlined |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> In round 8, Qareqare marked his return to the team with two tries against [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]];<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 April 2025 |title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 13-12 Castleford Tigers: Hosts edge win late on |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c367jedwp40o |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Cheshire |first=Adam |date=17 April 2025 |title=Wakefield Trinity end Belle Vue hoodoo against Castleford Tigers as Mason Lino drop goal seals stirring comeback triumph |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-end-belle-vue-hoodoo-against-castleford-tigers-as-mason-lino-drop-goal-seals-stirring-comeback-triumph-5089910 |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> the first of these was named Castleford's Try of the Season, his fourth such award.<ref>{{cite tweet |author=Castleford Tigers |user=CTRLFC |number=1967640716278104277 |title=A great night on Saturday for our player awards night}}</ref> However, in the final minute of the match, he suffered a recurrence of the hamstring injury.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=25 April 2025 |title='Really unfortunate timing': Castleford Tigers boss Danny McGuire reacts to major Jason Qareqare setback |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/really-unfortunate-timing-castleford-tigers-boss-danny-mcguire-reacts-to-major-jason-qareqare-setback-5099669 |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> This required surgery and necessitated a further four-month lay-off.<ref>{{Cite web |date=25 April 2025 |title=Tigers wing Qareqare facing up to four months out |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cd7vj0rejw1o |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=David |date=25 April 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers ace faces long lay-off after agonising double injury blow |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/castleford-tigers-jason-qareqare-injury-1113491 |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref> Though he returned to the Tigers' 21-man squad for the final two rounds, Qareqare was rested and made no further appearances.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=27 June 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers star likely to miss rest of season |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-star-set-to-see-specialist-following-concussion-worry-as-danny-mcguire-gives-damning-jason-qareqare-update/ |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=TotalRL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=19 September 2025 |title="Tired" Chris Chester admits Castleford Tigers star won't play against St Helens |url=https://www.totalrl.com/tired-chris-chester-admits-castleford-tigers-star-wont-play-against-st-helens/ |access-date=30 December 2025 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

In the opening months of the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]], Qareqare established a left-edge partnership with centre signing [[Krystian Mapapalangi]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=26 March 2026 |title=Ryan Carr hails Castleford's win over Bradford as decision to drop Mikaele Ravalawa explained |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ryan-carr-hails-castlefords-win-over-bradford-as-decision-to-drop-mikaele-ravalawa-explained/ |access-date=27 March 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=12 March 2026 |title=In Conversation: Jason Qareqare |url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article/233/in-conversation-jason-qareqare |access-date=27 March 2026 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He was named Tigers' player of the match against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[2026 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]],<ref>{{cite tweet |author=Castleford Tigers |user=CTRLFC |number=2032925370060537939 |title=Tigers POTM tonight: Jason Qareqare}}</ref> and scored his first career hat-trick in Castleford's 40–28 win against [[Bradford Bulls]] in round 6.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harby |first=Chris |date=26 March 2026 |title=Qareqare hat-trick fires Castleford past Bradford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cnv83618rrno |access-date=27 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=26 March 2026 |title=Qareqare heads Castleford charge as Bradford Bulls find themselves overrun |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/mar/26/castleford-bradford-super-league-rugby-league-match-report |access-date=27 March 2026 |website=The Guardian}}</ref> He scored a second hat-trick in Castleford's 30–10 victory over St Helens in round 12.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cartwright |first=Phil |date=23 May 2026 |title=Qareqare hat-trick helps Castleford beat St Helens |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/crmpdpmg38wo |access-date=23 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Qareqare represented [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]] in their Academy Origin victory against [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] on 4 August 2021. He played as a {{Rlp|wg}}er and scored the opening try of the game in the second minute.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 August 2021 |title=Yorkshire earn comfortable Academy Origin victory |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/35811/yorkshire-earn-comfortable-academy-origin-victory |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=The RFL}}</ref> He was named in the Yorkshire squad for the first match of the 2022 Academy Origin series, but did not take part due to [[Super League]] commitments.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=2 May 2022 |title=Jason Qareqare and Jack Sinfield among Super League starlets named in Origin Academy squads |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jason-qareqare-and-jack-sinfield-among-super-league-starlets-named-in-origin-academy-squads-3676890 |access-date=22 September 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2 May 2022 |title=Origin Academy squads unveiled |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37177/origin-academy-squads-unveiled |access-date=22 September 2023 |website=The RFL}}</ref>

In May 2022, Qareqare was named in [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]]'s initial 50-man squad in the build up towards the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Thomson |first=Doug |date=2022-05-29 |title=Castleford Tigers star Jason Qareqare among Super League stars named in Fiji squad |url=https://www.totalrl.com/tigers-star-qareqare-named-in-fiji-squad/ |access-date=2022-11-03 |website=Total Rugby League}}</ref> In September 2022, he was included in the extended 33-man training squad.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=2022-09-07 |title=Korbin Sims & Jason Qareqare included in Fiji's extended World Cup squad |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/korbin-sims-jason-qareqare-included-in-fijis-extended-world-cup-squad/ |access-date=2022-11-03 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> On 7 October 2022, Qareqare played on the {{Rlp|wg}} for Fiji against [[England national rugby league team|England]] in their warm-up game at the [[AJ Bell Stadium]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hafez |first=Shamoon |date=2022-10-07 |title=England 50-0 Fiji: Dominic Young stars on debut in Rugby League World Cup warm-up |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63179123 |access-date=2022-11-03 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-10-12 |title=Qareqare proud of Fiji debut |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8326 |access-date=2022-11-03 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> although missed out on a final selection in Fiji's 24-man tournament squad.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Thomson |first=Doug |date=2022-10-23 |title=Qareqare inspired to push on with Tigers |url=https://www.totalrl.com/qareqare-inspired-to-push-on-with-tigers/ |access-date=2022-11-03 |website=Total Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=2022-10-06 |title="Speechless" Jason Qareqare to fulfil dream of representing Fiji |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jason-qareqare-fiji-england-world-cup/ |access-date=2022-11-03 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

On 6 October 2023, Qareqare was named in [[2023 Pacific Rugby League Championships squads#Fiji|Fiji's 20-man squad]] for the [[2023 Pacific Rugby League Championships|2023 Pacific Championships]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=6 October 2023 |title=Super League duo called up to Fiji squad for Pacific Championships alongside NRL Grand Final winner |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-duo-jason-qareqare-king-vuniyayawa-called-up-fiji-squad-for-pacific-championships |access-date=7 October 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=6 October 2023 |title=Exciting rookie duo headline talented Fiji Pacific Championships squad |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/10/06/exciting-rookie-duo-headline-talented-fiji-squad/ |access-date=7 October 2023 |website=NRL.com}}</ref> He started in the second match on 29 October, scoring a try in Fiji's 43–16 win over {{rlnt|Papua New Guinea}}.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Tomlinson |first=Daniel |date=29 October 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers flyer Jason Qareqare scores eye-catching try in Fiji rout of PNG |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-flyer-jason-qareqare-28004806 |access-date=4 February 2024 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=29 October 2023 |title=PNG Kumuls 16 - 43 Fiji Bati |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/pacific-championships/2023/round-3/papua-new-guinea-v-fiji/ |access-date=4 February 2024 |website=NRL.com}}</ref> He featured in the Pacific Bowl final on 5 November where Fiji lost 12–32 against Papua New Guinea.<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 November 2023 |title=Fiji Bati 12 - 32 PNG Kumuls |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/pacific-championships/2023/grand-final/game-12/ |access-date=4 February 2024 |website=NRL.com}}</ref>
